macro_summary: Wednesday's session delivered a sharp reversal, with SPY falling 1.31% to $661.43 and QQQ dropping 1.37% to $594.90, erasing Tuesday's relief rally entirely as the Federal Reserve held rates steady and signalled no imminent cuts. Per Yahoo Finance, the Fed now projects core inflation at 2.7% by year-end — up from December's 2.5% forecast — while raising the 2026 GDP growth outlook to 2.4%. VIX surged 12.16% to 25.09, confirming the fear premium has reasserted itself after last week's brief compression window. The FOMC decision is scheduled for 14:00 ET today (March 19) and is the dominant catalyst for the session — any hawkish commentary in the press conference could accelerate the sell-off already underway in rate-sensitive tech names.
commodities_note: WTI crude settled at $96.57 (+0.21%), with the Strait of Hormuz crisis now in its 10th day — per AP News, roughly 89 ships crossed the strait between March 1-15 versus a pre-war baseline of 100-135 per day, with dark-vessel transits dominating. Gold fell 1.95% to $4,758.20, pulling back from recent highs but remaining structurally elevated on safe-haven demand. The DXY edged up 0.11% to 100.21, with dollar strength reflecting the rate-pause conviction. Gold at these levels is well above any clean entry point — GLD remains extended and unattractive for new positions despite the macro backdrop.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Would reconsider above SGD 57.59 (50-day average) on rising volume, or reassess below SGD 56.00 if NIM compression data worsens at the April 30 earnings release.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Becomes actionable on a pullback toward SGD 20.97 (50-day average), or if US10Y breaks below 4.20% — whichever comes first.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Upgrades to WATCH on a sustained close above SGD 37.36 with volume; reassess below SGD 35.93 (100-day average).

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. The Hormuz disruption remains a direct headwind for airline fuel costs; no entry until the 1-month downtrend reverses and price reclaims SGD 6.63.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.8% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 60 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Hard-blocked at 6.8% above its 50-day average with RSI at 60 — no entry. Would reconsider on a pullback to SGD 4.90-4.95 with volume confirmation.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA is the most important stock on this watchlist and right now it is technically broken — sitting 2.5% below a declining 50-day average of $185.09, with RSI at 45.6 in neutral-bearish territory. That matters because the Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis (per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B) is intact and compelling, but the stock is not acting like it. Wednesday's -1.07% move and the broader -1.31% SPY reversal confirm that Fed rate-pause multiple compression is overpowering the AI demand narrative right now. Per Yahoo Finance, CEO Jensen Huang projects sales north of $1 trillion through 2027 — a bullish call from a seller, and worth discounting accordingly. Fundamentally, the picture is actually attractive: forward PE of 16.4x is 11.8% below the semis cluster median, PEG of 1.1 is reasonable given 73.2% revenue growth, and analyst consensus is strong-buy with 95.6% projected earnings growth. The FCF yield of 1.33% is thin, however — the thesis leans heavily on continued revenue growth materialising. Key support is $177.41 (tested but not broken); the next floor is $169.81. Resistance at $184.47 then $194.67. A close above $184.47 with above-average volume and a stabilising VIX below 22 would be the upgrade trigger. Bottom line: NVDA is cheap relative to its growth rate, but the chart says the market isn't ready to reward it yet. Wait for the price to reclaim the 50-day average before entering.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD bucked Wednesday's selloff with a +1.69% gain, but at $199.46 it remains 7.1% below a declining 50-day average of $214.80 — the pre-filter correctly caps this at CAUTION. The fundamental case is arguably the strongest on the watchlist: 18.6x forward PE (exactly at cluster median), PEG of 0.58 with 34.1% revenue growth, and a confirmed Meta GPU supply deal for MI450-based Instinct GPUs per AMD's IR press release (Feb 24, 2026) — a multi-year, multi-generation partnership that validates AMD as a credible NVDA alternative at hyperscale. Additionally, per Simply Wall St. (March 19), Samsung Electronics announced a memorandum of understanding with AMD to expand AI memory collaboration including primary HBM4 supply for the Instinct MI455X and DDR5 for 6th Gen EPYC 'Venice' CPUs — this deepens AMD's access to critical AI memory and opens a second manufacturing partner. However, the 50-day average is declining and the 217.1% projected earnings growth is largely priced into the current recovery. FCF yield at 1.41% is thin, similar to NVDA. Next resistance is $218.84 then $265.85; nearest support is $158.03 then $149.47. Upgrade trigger: sustained close above $214.80 on above-average volume. AMD earnings are scheduled for approximately May 6 — a forward catalyst worth watching. Bottom line: AMD is the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to how fast it's growing, but the price trend is still pointing down. Wait for the 50-day average to flatten before entering.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 45x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel sits 2.9% below its 50-day average of $46.39 with RSI at 48.3 — technically not extreme, but the valuation is the problem. At 45.4x forward PE — 144% above the semis cluster median of 18.6x — Intel is priced for a sharp earnings recovery that hasn't materialised. Revenue growth is -4.1% year-on-year and FCF yield is negative (-2.0%), meaning the company is consuming cash. The pipeline soft-block correctly flags this: the 45x forward multiple requires explicit justification, and shrinking revenue makes that justification hard to construct. The one genuine catalyst is the 18A node: per Tom's Hardware (March 19 article citing Intel CFO David Zinsner at the Morgan Stanley TMT conference), yields are improving and CEO Lip-Bu Tan is now open to external customers for 18A — a meaningful shift from his earlier stance of treating it as an internal-only node. Additionally, per Simply Wall St. (March 19), Intel is co-developing 5G Advanced and 6G AI-RAN technologies with Viettel High Tech alongside AMD. Intel's earnings are approximately April 24. Resistance at $46.97 is the first gate; a clean break above that level on volume would signal the 18A foundry narrative is gaining traction. Until revenue turns positive, this stays on CAUTION regardless of the technology story. Bottom line: Intel has a genuine turnaround catalyst in the 18A node, but it's expensive for a company with shrinking revenues. Avoid until the April 24 earnings call clarifies the trajectory.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML fell 2.43% to $1,355.17, now 1.95% below its 50-day average of $1,382.08 — just inside the acceptable entry zone technically, but the fundamentals argue for patience. PEG of 2.26 is expensive: revenue growth of 4.9% against a 31.3x forward PE means you are paying a premium that the near-term growth rate does not justify, and the 68.3% premium to the semis cluster median is a significant overpayment without a near-term re-rating catalyst. FCF yield of 2.04% is marginal. The thesis remains intact longer-term — per Reuters (March 18), Belgian research lab imec has secured one of fewer than a dozen ASML High-NA EUV tools worldwide, and per the ASML Q4 2025 earnings call, the Intel 14A ramp in H2 2026 represents the next major revenue inflection. Earnings are approximately April 15 — within 27 days — which is a genuine binary event. A clean earnings beat with raised guidance would be the upgrade trigger. Support at $1,008.68 and $938.69 are the structural floors (the 200-day average is $1,018.21); resistance at $1,407.54 is the immediate ceiling. The Hormuz crisis and US-China trade tensions create ongoing macro headwinds per the active narrative. Bottom line: ASML is the best long-term holding in the semis cluster, but it's expensive for the growth rate it's currently delivering. Wait for the April 15 earnings to confirm the H2 2026 ramp before entering.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.3% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=8 (<30), RSI 64 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — price surged 16.3% above the 50-day average on blowout earnings, triggering a hard entry block that cannot be overridden. Micron reported record fiscal Q2 2026 results on March 18 (per the MU earnings release), confirming HBM demand and AI memory strength — the thesis is absolutely intact. The 5-day gain of 10.28% has pushed the stock to $461.73, well above the $396.92 50-day average. At 6.9x forward PE versus a cluster median of 18.6x (a 62.9% discount), Micron looks strikingly cheap — but that discount reflects the cyclical nature of memory pricing. FCF yield of 0.09% is nearly zero, suggesting cash generation hasn't yet caught up with the share price ramp. PEG of 0.64 with 56.7% revenue growth is genuinely compelling. Per Yahoo Finance (March 18), a CFRA analyst noted that Micron's margins might be peaking — worth monitoring. The 90-day return is 85.77% per Simply Wall St. (March 19). Nearest support is $363.90 then $357.67; no defined resistance — price is in discovery. The upgrade trigger is a pullback to the $363-370 range (approximately 20% below current price) on declining volume, which would bring it back within entry range. Bottom line: Micron just had its best earnings in years, but the stock has already priced in a lot of good news in a very short time. Wait for a meaningful pullback before entering.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon fell 2.33% to $209.87, now 5.56% below its declining 50-day average of $222.23 — technically broken with RSI at 45.3. The $200 billion AI capex plan (per AMZN's Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) is the defining tension: AWS revenue is accelerating and the Cerebras partnership for world's fastest AI inference on Amazon Bedrock (announced March 13 per Insider Monkey) is a genuine product catalyst, but the capex load initially sent shares down ~10% on missed Q1 operating income. Per Motley Fool (March 19), investor debate centres on when this capex converts to earnings. Forward PE of 22.5x with only 5.0% projected earnings growth gives a PEG of 1.97 — near the top of the neutral range and not cheap for a company in heavy investment mode. FCF yield at 1.06% is thin. The 50-day average is declining and below both the 100-day ($227.03) and 200-day ($224.80) averages — a technically weak structure. Support at $196.93 is the nearest floor. Upgrade trigger: close above $219.43 (near-term resistance) with volume, or a stabilisation of the capex narrative in Q1 earnings (approximately May 1). Bottom line: Amazon's cloud business is excellent, but heavy AI spending is compressing near-term profits and the chart is broken. Not attractive at current levels — wait for the May 1 earnings to reset expectations.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet fell 0.91% to $306.30, sitting 3.83% below its declining 50-day average of $318.50 with RSI at 46.7. The fundamental picture is reasonable — 22.8x forward PE (at the cluster median), PEG of 1.68 with 18% revenue growth, and 31.1% earnings growth consensus — but the technicals are structurally weak with both the 50-day and 200-day averages now above the current price. Per Reuters (March 18), Bridgewater's chief scientist Jasjeet Sekhon is joining DeepMind as chief strategy officer — a talent signal that reinforces Alphabet's AI positioning. Per Simply Wall St. (March 18), Alphabet is increasing its use of Chinese liquid cooling suppliers for data centre AI infrastructure, which introduces supply chain dependencies and potential geopolitical/regulatory risk worth monitoring given the US-China trade war escalation. FCF yield of 1.03% is thin. The AI valuation thesis is credible but requires the TPU v7 commercial momentum (per Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote) to convert to visible revenue. Earnings are approximately April 24. Support at $295.95 is the immediate floor. Upgrade trigger: reclaim of the $311.07 resistance level on volume ahead of or following the April 24 print. Bottom line: Google's AI business is real and growing, but the stock chart is uninspiring right now. Wait for the April 24 earnings or a clean technical breakout before entering.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +33.6% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 25% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — per Insider Monkey (March 19), Lumentum is confirmed to be joining the S&P 500 next week, and the stock surged 6.99% to $700.81 on continued inclusion buying, shifting the technical picture to a sustained structural trend rather than pure speculation. However, the entry block is absolute: at 33.6% above the 50-day average of $524.65, this is an extreme extension where no entry is viable regardless of the catalyst. The PEG of 0.15 looks anomalous — with a 48.2x forward PE, implied earnings growth would need to be extraordinary to justify a sub-0.2 PEG; treat this figure as unreliable and do not cite it as a valuation argument. FCF yield is essentially zero (-0.04%), meaning the thesis is entirely growth-dependent. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (per NVIDIA investor relations press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion are structural catalysts, not tactical ones. The nearest meaningful shorter-term support is $528.01 — a 24% pullback from current levels. Resistance is $783.80. No entry is viable until a significant pullback occurs. Bottom line: Lumentum has genuine structural tailwinds from NVDA's investment and index inclusion, but it's priced as if all the good news has already happened. Watch from the sidelines until a meaningful pullback brings it closer to $600-630.

COHR.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.7% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 55 (>=55).
COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Coherent gained 3.42% to $257.21 but remains hard-blocked at 13.7% above its 50-day average of $226.29. The PEG of 0.06 is anomalous — with a 35x forward PE, implied earnings growth would need to be implausibly high; do not cite this as a valuation argument. The 73% analyst-projected earnings growth and Stifel price target upgrade (per StockStory, March 18) citing AI datacenter positioning are genuine positives. The NVDA $2B silicon photonics investment (per NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) is confirmed and structural. Per Barron's (March 18), both LITE and COHR moved on Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's comments about continued copper cable use — a nuanced signal that optical and copper can coexist rather than being zero-sum. Support at $230.10 is the entry zone to watch. Upgrade trigger: pullback to $230-235 range on declining volume with RSI below 45. Bottom line: Coherent has real structural tailwinds from the NVDA partnership, but it needs to pull back at least 10% before the risk-reward makes sense. Watch for a test of the $230 support level.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 73 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. XLE is an ETF — skip PEG, FCF, and cluster-relative checks. Hard-blocked at 11% above its 50-day average of $52.65 with RSI at 72.6 (overbought), this is one of the clearest avoid signals on the watchlist. The Hormuz disruption (now day 10) supports the energy premium, but RSI at overbought levels historically precede mean-reversion pullbacks regardless of the fundamental driver. WTI at $96.57 provides the underlying support, but the ETF is already pricing in a significant geopolitical premium. Support at $43.08 and $41.61 are too far away to be actionable near-term reference points. Upgrade trigger (from Caution to Watch): a pullback to approximately $54-55 range (near the 50-day average) with RSI cooling below 55 would create a cleaner entry, roughly equivalent to WTI sustaining above $88. Bottom line: Energy is the right sector in this geopolitical environment, but XLE has already run hard. Avoid entering now — wait for either a pullback or a new escalation that hasn't been priced in yet.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 82x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Palantir is sitting almost exactly on its 50-day average of $152.67 (price $152.77, 0.07% above) with RSI at 54.3 — a technically neutral setup. The valuation is extreme by any measure: 81.8x forward PE is 258.8% above the BigTech cluster median of 22.8x, PEG of 3.02, and FCF yield of 0.35% — but the catalysts are real. Per the government contract databases, Palantir holds a $10 billion Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) plus a confirmed $448 million Navy ShipOS deal per DoD press release. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200 and Daiwa upgraded to Buy with a $180 target per analyst notes. The 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY) per company guidance is the anchor for the bull case. However, the entry block correctly flags the 82x forward PE as requiring explicit justification — the 647.6% projected earnings growth (if real) is the justification, but that figure needs to be validated by consecutive quarters. The soft-block means HOLD is appropriate: the thesis is intact but the valuation demands ongoing validation. Earnings approximately May 5. Resistance at $187.08 then $190.19; support at $126.30 then $119.17. Bottom line: Palantir is a defence AI leader with real government contracts, but at over 80 times forward earnings you are paying a very high price for that story. Hold if you already have exposure to the thesis — this is not the entry point for new money.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider on a close above the 50-day average of $132.14, or if VIX drops sustainably below 20 signalling a broader risk-on reset.

singapore.summary: The cluster holds a split HOLD/WATCH/CAUTION posture with no signal upgrades today. The structural thesis hasn't changed: NIM compression from the Fed's rate pause is the primary headwind for DBS, OCBC, and UOB, while Singtel and SIA face their own macro pressures. The mean-reversion trigger — US10Y below 4.20% and STI above 5,000 — is moving in the wrong direction with the 10-year now at 4.26%.
singapore.key_development: US10Y rising to 4.26% (vs. 4.22% Tuesday) confirms the rate-pause thesis is entrenching rather than softening, extending the timeline for NIM compression relief.

semiconductors.summary: The cluster is deeply split: MU's blowout earnings (record revenue, HBM demand confirmed) triggered a signal change to CAUTION due to extreme extension, while NVDA and AMD remain technically broken below declining 50-day averages. ASML and INTC sit just below their respective 50-day averages but face valuation headwinds. The divergence between strong fundamentals and weak technicals across the cluster reflects Fed multiple compression dominating the AI demand narrative.
semiconductors.key_development: MU's signal change from WATCH to CAUTION after a 16.3% run above its 50-day average on blowout earnings crystallises the core cluster tension: best fundamentals, worst entry point.

big_tech.summary: All three names (AMZN, GOOG, PLTR) are below or at their 50-day averages with structurally declining trend lines on the first two. PLTR is the standout given its defence AI catalysts and neutral technical position, but its valuation remains the most extreme on the watchlist. AMZN and GOOG face the same problem: real AI businesses, but chart structures that aren't rewarding buyers.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — PLTR stable, AMZN/GOOG technically broken
big_tech.key_development: AMZN's 5.56% extension below its 50-day average (now also below both the 100-day and 200-day averages) marks the weakest technical structure in the BigTech cluster.

photonics.summary: LITE's confirmed S&P 500 inclusion next week drove a signal reclassification from CAUTION to HOLD — the structural catalyst is real, but the 33.6% extension above the 50-day average makes the entry impossible. COHR remains hard-blocked at 13.7% above. The cluster is in a period of thesis validation without entry opportunity — both names need to pull back meaningfully before they become actionable.
photonics.key_development: LITE's S&P 500 inclusion confirmed for next week (per Insider Monkey, March 19) — the inclusion-driven buying cycle is now in its final phase, after which passive rebalancing demand fades.

energy.summary: XLE remains the clearest 'avoid' on the watchlist — overbought at RSI 72.6 and 11% above the 50-day average. The Hormuz crisis (day 10) justifies energy sector exposure in principle, but the ETF has already priced in a substantial geopolitical premium. The right trade for energy is watching for a pullback, not chasing.
energy.key_development: Per AP News, roughly 89 ships crossed the Strait between March 1-15 versus 100-135 pre-war — traffic recovering slightly but still deeply disrupted, sustaining the supply premium.

index.summary: VUAA.L sits 2.77% below its 50-day average at $128.48, with RSI at 40.3 approaching oversold territory. The S&P 500 technical breakdown narrative (now day 10) remains the dominant theme — SPY at $661.43 is testing the $660-665 support range. No change to CAUTION.
index.key_development: SPY's 1.31% decline on Wednesday's FOMC reaction puts the critical $660-665 support range under active test; a sustained break below $658 would deepen the breakdown.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM)
interconnected.reason: NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp and AMD's MI455X production both run through TSMC fabs — utilisation rates and pricing power are direct read-throughs for the semis cluster.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, or after AMD's May 6 earnings confirm MI455X volume production timelines.

interconnected.name: Broadcom (AVGO)
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC demand from hyperscalers (Google TPU, Meta) is Broadcom's primary growth driver — a direct read-through from GOOG and AMZN's AI capex trajectories.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if GOOG's April 24 earnings confirm accelerating TPU v7 deployment, which would validate Broadcom's custom silicon revenue trajectory.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Primary HBM supplier to NVDA and now being displaced at AMD's MI455X by Samsung — the AMD-Samsung HBM4 MOU announced March 18 is a direct competitive threat to SK Hynix's dominant HBM position.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting as a watchlist addition if SK Hynix shares pull back 12-15% on AMD-Samsung HBM4 supply-share concerns, creating a mean-reversion entry.

active_risks: Iran conflict: Strait of Hormuz traffic collapsed to ~6 vessels/day from 138 baseline; WTI holding near $97 with analyst $100-135 trajectory if conflict extends beyond 4 months
US-China trade war: 100% tariffs on China imports plus rare earth export controls reshaping semiconductor supply chains; China restricting export of materials critical for chip manufacturing
Fed rate pause: Sticky inflation (core PCE 2.7% projected year-end) extending the rate-pause cycle, compressing tech multiples and pressuring rate-sensitive Singapore banks
US tariff policy uncertainty: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150-day window) replacing struck-down IEEPA tariffs; November 1 tariff escalation date flagged by Trump
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz disruption continues to support XLE's overbought rally (RSI 72.6) while directly pressuring SIA (C6L_SI) through elevated fuel costs — the airline remains on HOLD with no clean entry. The Fed's hawkish pause is the dominant equity market force, with the US10Y rising to 4.26% compressing multiples across NVDA, AMD, AMZN, and GOOG, all of which remain on CAUTION despite intact underlying AI demand narratives.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Per Yahoo Finance, the FOMC decision today confirmed rates held steady with the SEP showing core inflation revised up to 2.7% (from 2.5% in December) — more hawkish than consensus expected. Per AP News, Trump issued a temporary waiver of US shipping law requirements in a bid to fight soaring energy prices.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay in cash on new positions — VIX at 25 and the Fed's upward inflation revision create an unfavourable entry environment; wait for VIX to settle below 20 and SPY to reclaim $670 before deploying capital.

events_this_week.event: FOMC Rate Decision (14:00 ET) — Fed press conference with Powell
Micron (MU) Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings — record results already reported; market reaction ongoing
Lumentum (LITE) S&P 500 inclusion effective — passive rebalancing demand peaks then fades
ASML Q1 2026 earnings — could resolve CAUTION signal if guidance confirms High-NA EUV ramp acceleration
Intel (INTC) Q1 2026 earnings — 18A node yield progress and revenue trajectory are the key signal-change variables
Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 2026 earnings — TPU v7 commercial revenue and cloud growth rate are the upgrade triggers
DBS (D05_SI) earnings — NIM trajectory and dividend guidance will determine if HOLD upgrades to WATCH
AMD MI450 Meta supply deal first 1GW shipments — per AMD IR press release Feb 24, 2026; validates hyperscale GPU diversification away from NVDA
Intel 14A High-NA EUV customer ramp — per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call; critical revenue inflection for both INTC and ASML

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Fed's upward inflation revision to 2.7% core year-end combined with VIX at 25 leaves no attractive entry points across 12 of 17 watchlist tickers — the single Watch name (O39_SI) requires a macro reset that is moving in the wrong direction.

watch_for_entry.note: Closest to actionable — only 2.44% above its 50-day average. Enter on pullback to SGD 20.97 or on US10Y breaking below 4.20%.

on_deck.note: Forward PE 16.4x — 11.8% below semis median — with 73% revenue growth. Fundamentally attractive but technically broken.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.1 with 95.6% projected earnings growth; cheapest quality large-cap in the semis cluster on a growth-adjusted basis. Revenue growth (73.2%) far exceeds the 16.4x forward PE. FCF yield thin at 1.33% but earnings ramp is the story.
on_deck.trigger: VIX drops below 20 AND NVDA closes above $184.47 (first resistance) with above-average volume on a day when SPY is up more than 1%.

on_deck.note: PEG 0.58 with 34% revenue growth — the best growth-adjusted value on the watchlist. Meta GPU deal (per AMD IR) is the multi-year anchor.
on_deck.rationale: 18.6x forward PE at cluster median with 217% projected earnings growth and confirmed Meta MI450 hyperscale deployment. Samsung HBM4 MOU deepens supply chain resilience. No fundamental thesis deterioration — CAUTION is macro-driven.
on_deck.trigger: AMD 50-day average ($214.80) flattens and price reclaims $210 on above-average volume with semis sector broadly recovering.

caution_trim.note: Avoid. 45.4x forward PE with -4.1% revenue growth and negative FCF yield — valuation unjustified by current fundamentals regardless of 18A optionality.
Avoid for now. PEG 2.26 is expensive for 4.9% near-term revenue growth — wait for April 15 earnings to confirm H2 2026 High-NA ramp.
Avoid at current levels. Hard-blocked 16.3% above 50-day average after blowout earnings — needs 15-20% pullback to $363-370 range.
Avoid. Structurally broken below all major averages; $200B capex plan compressing near-term earnings with only 5% growth consensus.
Avoid. Below declining 50-day average with Chinese supply chain dependency risk emerging; wait for April 24 earnings.
Avoid. 33.6% above 50-day average — extreme extension with zero FCF yield; S&P 500 inclusion buying fades after next week.
Avoid. Hard-blocked 13.7% above 50-day average; wait for pullback to $230-235 support range.
Avoid. RSI 72.6 (overbought) and 11% above 50-day average — Hormuz premium fully priced in at current levels.
Avoid. Hard-blocked 6.8% above 50-day average with RSI 60 — no entry until pullback to SGD 4.90-4.95.
Avoid. S&P 500 technical breakdown ongoing; SPY testing critical $660-665 support with no recovery signal.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH above SGD 57.59 with volume. Reassess below SGD 56.00 ahead of April 30 earnings.
Upgrades to WATCH on sustained close above SGD 37.36. Reassess below SGD 35.93.
No action while Hormuz disruption persists. Upgrades to Watch on close above SGD 6.63 with sector recovery.
Upgrades to Watch above $187.08 resistance on volume. Reassess below $126.30 if defence AI spending narrative weakens.

new_stocks_to_watch.note: TSMC is the critical fab for NVDA Blackwell Ultra and AMD MI455X — the purest way to own the AI compute ramp without betting on a single chip designer.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Broadcom's custom AI ASIC revenue is a direct read-through from GOOG and AMZN AI capex — less valuation risk than pure-play AI names.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Revisit after GOOG April 24 earnings confirm TPU v7 deployment scale

new_stocks_to_watch.note: AMD-Samsung HBM4 MOU (March 18) is a competitive threat to SK Hynix's dominant HBM position — monitor for a pullback creating a mean-reversion entry.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Becomes interesting if 000660.KS pulls back 12-15% on AMD-Samsung supply-share concerns

contrarian_candidates.watching_for: SPY reclaims $665 on above-average volume with VIX dropping below 22 — would signal tactical bottom rather than structural breakdown continuation.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Powell signals rate cuts possible by mid-2026 if inflation data improves; tone less hawkish than SEP suggests
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → On Deck upgraded (multiple compression reverses)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Powell reinforces 2.7% inflation outlook, signals no cuts in 2026 and possibly one hike
